You will work directly with the attorney to ensure client matters move smoothly from engagement to completion—while also helping to refine and document our processes as we grow. The right candidate will enjoy creating order, learning independently, and taking initiative.



While the role will start at 15–20 hours per week, it has clear potential to expand into a full- time position with greater responsibility.



Core Responsibilities



Client Coordination & Communication



Serve as the main point of contact for clients across estate planning, real estate, mediation, and business matters.

Conduct 15-minute discovery/intake calls and manage scheduling for client meetings, signings, and mediations.

Guide clients through pre-meeting preparation (forms, portals, document uploads, and next steps).

Communicate clearly and professionally via phone,
, and Slack, maintaining a warm, reassuring tone.

Track progress in Lawmatics and Google Workspace, ensuring deadlines, notes, and follow-ups are current.

Coordinate virtual or in-person signing logistics (notary, witnesses, delivery of executed documents).

Support ongoing client communication and check-ins between meetings.





Process Development & Workflow Management



Collaborate with the attorney to build, refine, and document firm workflows for all practice areas.

Create internal checklists, templates, and automation within Lawmatics.

Identify opportunities to improve efficiency, reduce redundancy, and enhance client experience.

Learn independently from written or video resources before seeking clarification, showing initiative and curiosity.

Help organize and maintain the firm’s operations and procedures manual.





Marketing & Outreach Support



Assist with social media posting and engagement on platforms like
, Instagram, and YouTube.

Help prepare and send the firm’s weekly e-newsletter, including light editing, scheduling, and contact management.

Maintain referral partner and contact lists in Lawmatics.

Support the planning of community events (e.g., “Wine & Wills,” presentations, and expos).

Track basic marketing metrics (registrations, event attendance, new client leads).





(*Note: Most physical office tasks, such as scanning or binder assembly, will be handled by another tea
mber.)



Key Skills & Qualities



Strong written and verbal communication skills—clear, confident, and client-friendly.

Highly organized with meticulous attention to detail.

Tech-savvy and comfortable using Lawmatics, Google Drive, Docs, and Sheets.

Independent learner: capable of finding and applying information from provided resources.

Process-oriented: enjoys improving systems and building structure.

Professional and compassionate demeanor, especially when working with families or sensitive matters.

Reliable, self-motivated, and adaptable in a small firm environment.





Preferred Experience



2+ years of experience supporting a law firm

Background in real estate, estate planning, or mediation/family law preferred.

Experience coordinating files, clients, or project workflows.

Experience in Lawmatics

Notary Public in NY (or willingness to obtain) is a plus.

Comfort with social media platforms and light marketing tasks.

title: Senior AWS DevOps Engineer – AWS Cost Optimization & Kubernetes
description:
Job Description:

About the Role

We're a blockchain/DeFi company running production infrastructure that's bleeding money unnecessarily. Our AWS bill is sitting at $4,500/month – and we know it shouldn't be anywhere near that. We need someone who can come in, audit the whole setup, and actually fix it. Not just give us a report that sits in a folder.



We've already been through a few candidates who talked a big game but couldn't execute. We're done with that. If you're the type who needs handholding or can't work independently, this isn't for you.





What You'll Actually Do

- Audit our entire AWS infrastructure and identify exactly where we're wasting money (we expect significant savings)

- Optimize our Kubernetes clusters – right-size nodes, fix resource requests/limits, implement autoscaling that actually works

- Implement cost controls: Reserved Instances, Savings Plans, spot instances where appropriate, and cleanup of orphaned resources

- Set up monitoring and alerts so costs don't creep back up without us knowing

- Document everything you do so the team can maintain it





Hard Requirements (Non-Negotiable)

- 3+ years hands-on Kubernetes experience in production environments – EKS specifically preferred

- Deep AWS knowledge: EC2, EKS, RDS, S3, CloudWatch, Cost Explorer, Trusted Advisor

- Proven track record of actually reducing AWS costs (be ready to share numbers/percentages)

- Strong scripting skills (Python, Bash) for automation

- Infrastructure as Code: Terraform or CloudFormation (Terraform preferred)

- Self-starter who can take ownership and execute without constant oversight





!!!!CRITICAL: This Role Requires Surgical Precision

This is production infrastructure running live systems. Think of this like surgery – one wrong move can take everything down.

- NO cowboy moves. You don't delete, modify, or remove anything without a clear plan first.

- Every change must be planned out, documented, and confirmed with the CEO before execution.

- Extreme attention to detail. You double-check and triple-check before touching anything.

- If you're not 100% sure what something does, you ask first. You don't assume.

- Methodical execution only. We value careful and correct over fast and reckless.





Strong Plus

- Experience with blockchain/Web3 infrastructure

- Karpenter, Cluster Autoscaler, or KEDA experience

-  AWS certifications (Solutions Architect, DevOps Engineer)

- Experience with FinOps tools (Kubecost, CloudHealth, 
)





What We Need In Your Application

Don't send a generic application. We will ignore it. Include:

- Specific examples of AWS cost optimization you've done – what was the setup, what did you change, what % did you save?

- Your Kubernetes experience – cluster sizes you've managed, scaling strategies you've implemented

- Your availability and timezone (we need someone who can overlap with US business hours for some calls)





Compensation & Structure

- $20-25/hour based on experience and proven results

- 20 hours/week to start – potential for more hours for the right person

- Fully remote

- Initial project: Comprehensive audit + implementation plan with clear cost reduction targets





If you're someone who can actually deliver results and not just talk about them – we want to hear from you. Show us what you've done.

title: Social Media Manager for U.S. Real Estate Brand
description:
We’re looking for a Social Media Manager with real experience in U.S. real estate.



This is not for a generic social media person. You need to understand how the real estate market works in the United States, know how to work with MLS listings, and know how to create content that builds trust, attracts the right audience, and supports lead generation.



You should understand the difference between just posting content and creating content that helps a real estate brand stay relevant, credible, and visible in a competitive market.



What we need:



We’re looking for someone who can support content creation and social media management for a real estate brand, including:



Creating and organizing social media content for Instagram and other platforms



Writing captions and post copy related to listings, buyer and seller education, market insights, and brand positioning



Turning MLS listings into clean, engaging, marketable social media content



Understanding how to present properties in a way that feels strategic, polished, and appealing



Planning content that builds trust and helps generate inbound leads



Supporting a consistent brand presence across social platforms



To be a fit, you should have:



Experience working with real estate businesses in the United States



Experience using or working with MLS listings



Understanding of U.S. real estate terminology, processes, and audience behavior



Experience creating social media content for real estate agents, brokers, teams, or brokerages



Strong copywriting and content planning skills



Ability to create content that is not generic, outdated, or overly salesy





You likely understand:



how to market listings properly



how to speak to buyers, sellers, and investors



how to use social media to build trust, not just push listings



what kind of content performs in real estate

title: Digital Templating Operator (PhotoModeler ? VCarve) — Simple Shapes, SOP?Driven
description:
ABOUT THE ROLE



Windows Well Covered (Denver, USA) needs one reliable operator to follow a simple, repeatable SOP:

- Import photos to PhotoModeler and generate a basic model.

- Export; import to VCarve.

- Verify/resize to the provided measurements.

- Create a single?layer cutpath (no multi?layer/complex CAD).

- Save toolpaths and VCarve file; upload with strict naming & folder rules to OneDrive.



We value accuracy, naming discipline, and deadlines over advanced CAD. Training is a 30–40 min video; the first task must be done using the video only.





PAY (PIECEWORK)



- $1.50 per cut path file for the first 500 created in a calendar year

- $2.00 per cut path file for 501–1000 in a calendar year

- $2.50 per cut path file for 1000+ in a calendar year



Accuracy bonus: $50 every 100 perfect templates (no redos) (? +$0.50/template on streaks)



Loyalty bonus: Each new year on contract raises your starting rate by +$0.25/template (year resets Jan 1)





SERVICE LEVEL AGREEMENT



24 hours per job assignment





WORK VOLUME



- Busy Season (Late April - Early November): Approximately 40–60 per week

- Slow Season (Late November - Early April): Approximately 10–20/week

*top performers may receive a small slow?season retainer



Hourly equivalents (for expectation setting):

At 10–12 min/cut path file ? $12–$18/hr with accuracy bonus (higher when faster: 8–9 min ? ~$15–$22/hr).



Preferred locations: Philippines, Vietnam. Others may apply.





REQUIREMENTS:



- Clear written English.

- Windows 10 or 11 (native installation). MacOS or Linux are not supported for this workflow.

- PC: i5/Ryzen 5+, 16 GB RAM, SSD, 1080p+. (Windows OS Required)

- Internet: ? 25 Mbps down / 10 Mbps up (attach speed test taken during your planned work hours).

- Familiarity with PhotoModeler or VCarve is a plus (not required if you learn quickly).

- Extreme attention to naming conventions, folder structure, and on?time delivery.





HOW TO APPLY (required):



- 2–3 min Loom showing how you follow a checklist on your computer.

- Take a screenshot of ‘About This PC’ (Windows specs page) and upload with their audition files.

- Screen shot of speed test uploaded.

- Confirm 1 hour overlap with America/Denver for handoffs/feedback if needed.

- Briefly note any PhotoModeler/VCarve/CNC/vector?tracing experience (ok if none).

- Please confirm comfort with piecework and seasonal volume.

- Please confirm you are using a Windows 10 or 11 PC (native, not virtualized). Include your version (e.g., Windows 11 Pro).



LOOM VIDEO DIRECTIONS



- 2–3 min Loom video requirement

- Record your screen while you demonstrate how you follow this checklist below:

    - Create a folder on your desktop called “WWC Test Audition”

    - Inside, make 2 subfolders. One titled “Projects Assigned” the other one “Projects Completed”

    - Inside “Projects Assigned” create a new subfolder called “Customer Name (2 covers)”

    - Save 2 blank text files in each folder each named respectively:  “CustomerName1”, “CustomerName2”

    - Then move the Customer Name folder from “Projects Assigned” to “Projects Completed”

    - Show your thought process out loud: narrate what you’re doing, why you’re naming files that way, and confirm against the checklist.

    - Close by showing the final folder structure on your screen and ticking each item off the checklist.



Important:

- Speak clearly in English (doesn’t have to be perfect).

- Keep the video under 3 minutes.

- Share the Loom link in your proposal.

title: Turn Telegram Contacts into $5,000/Month: DeFi Protocol Needs BD Now
description:

We’re a DeFi protocol launching game-changing LP bond technology. We need Business Development partners who can introduce us to crypto launchpads, blockchain projects, and DeFi communities.





THE OPPORTUNITY:

Every successful introduction that leads to partnership earns you serious money. One good partnership could net you $5,000-10,000 in your first month.





COMPENSATION:

Monetize your network through three massive income streams: guaranteed ETH salary, instant token unlocks, and recurring commissions. We pay you to open doors you’ve already built, while we handle 100% of the closing.



Guaranteed Base Pay: You will receive $10/hour (capped at 10 hours/week),       for simply activating your network. 50% paid in Ethereum (ETH) for immediate cash and 50% in $GMI tokens, which are subject to an 18-month vesting schedule, building a long-term position in the $GMI mutli-chain ecosystem.



Performance Bonus: Every 5 successful partnership you close triggers an immediate unlock of 20% your vested tokens. This allows you to bypass the 18-month vesting period and access your earnings as soon as you deliver results.

Note: A "successful partnership" is defined by the project signing the LP Bonds integration agreement and going live on our marketplace.



Additional Commission: 

- 25% commission on ALL upgrade fees from your partnerships

- Level 2 upgrades: $40 upgrade fee, you get $10 each

- Level 3 upgrades: $80 upgrade fee, you get $20 each

- Level 4 upgrades: $160 upgrade fee, you get $40 each



-  $500 bonus when your partnerships hit 25 Level 4 bonds

-  $2,000 bonus at 100 Level 4 bonds

-  $5,000 bonus at 300 Level 4 bonds

-  Realistic month 1 earnings: $2,000-5,000

- Total 90-day potential: $20,000+



YOU HAVE:

- Existing connections in crypto (launchpads, DAOs, blockchain projects)

-
/
 relationships with project founders or marketing heads

- Understanding of DeFi/crypto space

- Hunger to earn based on results, not hours





YOU DON’T NEED:

- To close deals (I handle that)

- Any upfront investment

- Previous BD experience (connections matter more)

- Full-time commitment (work your own schedule)





THE ROLE:

Simply introduce me to decision-makers at crypto projects. I close the deals, you collect commissions. Every user from your partnerships who upgrades their bonds puts money in your pocket..

Example: ONE medium-sized launchpad partnership generating 100 L4 bonds = $8,000 for you.



Ready to turn your crypto connections into serious income?



Apply with:

- List 3-5 crypto projects/launchpads you have connections with

- Your
 handle

- Brief description of your crypto involvement



We need partners who see the opportunity and want to run with it. Start earning within days of your first successful introduction!

title: Hard Sales Representative / Closer – Remote
description:
About the Role:

We are looking for a high-performing, results-driven Sales Representative who is confident on calls, thrives under targets, and knows how to close. This is not a passive customer service role — this is a high-activity, hard-sales position focused on converting leads, handling objections, and driving revenue.



If you are competitive, resilient, and motivated by performance and commissions, we want to hear from you.



Key Responsibilities:

* Conduct outbound sales calls and follow-ups

* Close warm and cold leads

* Handle objections and push deals forward

* Hit daily call and sales targets

* Manage pipeline and update CRM

* Book and close appointments

* Follow structured sales scripts and processes

* Maintain consistent follow-ups

* Track and report sales performance



KPIs & Expectations:

* High daily call volume

* Consistent outreach activity

* Conversion and close rates

* Revenue generated

* CRM accuracy

* Follow-up discipline

* This role requires urgency, persistence, and confidence on the phone.



Qualifications:

* 2+ years in sales, closing, or hard outbound roles

* Strong English communication skills

* Neutral or American accent is a strong plus

* Comfortable with cold calling and rejection

* Proven track record of hitting targets

* Competitive and results-oriented mindset

* Coachable and able to follow scripts/processes

* Able to work US business hours



Technical Requirements:

* Computer (8GB RAM or higher)

* Noise-canceling headset

* Webcam

* Stable high-speed internet

* Quiet workspace

* Backup internet/power preferred



How to Apply?

Reply to this job post with:

* UPDATED Resume

* UPDATED professional intro video (1–2 minutes)

* In your video, please include:

* Your hard sales / closing experience

* What products or services you’ve sold

* Your average daily call or outreach numbers

* Your sales results (appointments, revenue, conversions, etc.)

* How you handle objections and rejection

* One fun fact about yourself

* Why you’re confident you can hit targets in a high-performance sales role



This role is for strong salespeople who are confident, consistent, and ready to perform. If you’re driven by targets, not afraid of rejection, and serious about closing deals, we’d love to meet you.

title: Senior Podcast QA Manager/ Podcast Production Manager
description:
We are a rapidly growing US-based podcast production and marketing agency looking for a Senior Podcast QA Manager/ Podcast Production Manager.



This is not just an editing role. We are looking for someone who can help oversee podcast production across multiple client accounts, maintain high quality standards, keep projects organized, stay ahead of deadlines, and bring structure to a fast-paced agency environment.



This role is more focused on top-notch quality control and oversight than pure execution, although there will absolutely be times when you need to jump in and get things done yourself. You should be someone who has a strong eye for quality, catches issues early, gives clear feedback, and helps raise the standard of the team.



You should be proactive, highly organized, calm under pressure, and able to take real ownership of your work. We want someone who does not wait until the last minute, does not need constant follow-up, and knows how to keep moving parts on track.



If you want to grow quickly, take on more responsibility, and build your career within a stable and growing environment, this is the perfect position for you.



Rate and Hours

• $5 USD/hour

• 20 hours per week to start

• Strong opportunity for long-term growth and advancement within the agency



What You’ll Be Responsible For

• Overseeing podcast production workflows across multiple client accounts

• Reviewing audio, show notes, titles, thumbnails, and deliverables for quality and accuracy

• Giving feedback and mentorship to editors, podcast managers, and tea
mbers

• Helping maintain and improve quality standards across the agency

• Editing podcast audio and video if needed (we already have editors on the team)

• Reviewing and improving show notes and episode titles

• Using good judgment to identify what works and what does not in titles, YouTube thumbnails, and content presentation

• Scheduling episodes and helping make sure publishing stays on track

• Assisting with monthly planning and upcoming content organization

• Tracking deadlines, deliverables, and team responsibilities

• Identifying problems early and communicating them clearly

• Helping organize internal systems and workflows

• Making sure client work moves forward without last-minute chaos

• Supporting a smooth and reliable delivery process across the agency



You Must Have

• Previous agency experience, ideally in podcasting, content marketing, media, or client services

• Strong experience with podcast production workflows

• Experience editing audio and video

• Strong writing skills for show notes and titles

• A strong eye for quality, detail, and what makes content perform well

• Good judgment when reviewing titles, thumbnails, and overall content packaging

• Experience giving feedback, reviewing work, and helping others improve

• Strong project management and organizational ability

• Ability to manage multiple clients, deadlines, and moving parts at once

• Ability to work ahead and not leave important work until the last minute

• Clear and proactive communication

• A strong sense of ownership and accountability



This Role Is Best For Someone Who

• Takes ownership instead of needing constant reminders

• Naturally stays organized and likes keeping things on track

• Has high standards and genuinely cares about quality

• Is dependable and follows through

• Can manage deadlines in a fast-paced environment

• Thinks ahead and spots issues before they become problems

• Can give direct, helpful feedback that improves the team’s work

• Wants long-term growth and more responsibility over time

• Is proud of doing excellent work and being part of a strong team



This Role Is Not For Someone Who

• Misses deadlines or works at the last minute

• Needs to be chased for updates

• Gets overwhelmed easily by multiple moving parts

• Applies without reading the full post

• Has no agency experience

• Is disorganized, reactive, or inconsistent

• Is only comfortable doing tasks, but not reviewing and improving the work of others



How to Apply

To be considered, please send:



• Your CV / resume

• Your portfolio

• Your website, if you have one

• A short message explaining your agency experience

• Examples of podcasts or content you have worked on

• The tools you use for audio editing, video editing, and content repurposing

• A short note explaining how you stay organized when managing multiple deadlines and clients

• A short note explaining how you approach quality control and feedback when reviewing someone else’s work



Please also include the phrase “I love podcasts” at the top of your application.



Applications that do not follow the instructions above will not be considered.



We are looking for someone who wants to grow with a serious agency, take on real responsibility, and become a key part of a long-term team.

title: Social Media Support
description:

title: Client Onboarding & Tech VA — Remote (Full-Time)
description:
We're Tripathi Consulting — a growth consultancy helping auto detailing shops across North America scale their high-ticket services (PPF, Ceramic Coating, Tint, Wraps). We build AI-powered marketing and sales systems that generate qualified leads, automate follow-ups, and fill our clients' calendars with $2K–$5K jobs.



We're looking for a Client Onboarding & Tech VA to join our team full-time. This is NOT a task-based VA role — we're looking for someone who wants to grow into a real career inside a fast-scaling agency.



You'll start by owning client onboarding and tech setup, but as you prove yourself, your scope will expand. We invest in our team's development and want you learning, improving, and taking on new challenges every month.



The responsibilities listed here are your starting point — not your ceiling.



WHAT YOU'LL OWN:



Client Onboarding (Core of the Role)

- Set up new client sub-accounts in GoHighLevel

- Handle A2P registration and domain configuration

- Build out forms, calendars,
 systems, and automations from templates

- Ensure every new client is fully set up and ready before their first team call



CRM / GHL Buildouts & Maintenance

- Build and maintain workflows, pipelines, and automations inside GHL

- Troubleshoot issues and keep the backend clean as we scale clients

- Implement updates and improvements across client accounts



Ad Creative Production

- Design ad graphics in Canva using brand templates

- Edit short-form video for ads using CapCut

- Produce creative assets on demand for the media buying team



Internal Ops & Documentation

- Keep ClickUp updated with task progress and daily status

- Record Loom walkthroughs for completed work and new SOPs

- Help document processes so the team can scale efficiently



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR:

- Strong English — written and spoken, clear communication is essential

- Tech-savvy — comfortable learning new platforms quickly (GHL, ClickUp, Canva, CapCut)

- Detail-oriented — every client setup needs to be right the first time

- Self-starter — you don't wait to be told what to do, you figure it out

- Hungry to learn — you want to grow your skills, not just collect a paycheck

- Reliable — you show up every day, hit deadlines, and follow through



TOOLS YOU'LL USE:

- GoHighLevel, Canva, CapCut, ClickUp, Slack, Google Workspace, Loom



COMPENSATION:

- $640–$800 USD/month depending on experience

- Pay increases tied directly to performance, skill growth, and expanded responsibilities

- As you take on more, you earn more — we review compensation regularly



GROWTH PATH:

This role is a launchpad, not a dead end:



Start ? Onboarding & Tech VA (client setup, CRM builds, ad creative, internal ops)

3–8 months ? Senior VA / Ops Lead (client management, process ownership, training new hires)

8–15 months ? Operations Manager (department oversight, strategy input, team leadership)



Your responsibilities will grow past this scope as you develop. We challenge our team in new areas and make sure you're always progressing — not plateauing.



HOURS & COMMUNICATION:

- Full-Time, Monday–Friday (flexible within EST business hours)

- All communication via Slack + ClickUp

- Must have fast, reliable internet connection



HIRING PROCESS:

- Application review

- Group interview with shortlisted candidates

- Paid trial period (3–5 days)

- Full-time offer



APPLY HERE: 



We're scaling fast and looking for A-players who want to grow with us. If that's you — apply now.

title: Industrial Electrical Estimator (Conest Intellibid)
description:
JOB OVERVIEW:

Vertical Electric Inc specializes in high-precision cost estimating for industrial electrical infrastructure in California. We are seeking a long-term, full-time, extremely detail-oriented Industrial Electrical Estimator.



This is not a commercial or residential role; you will be responsible for developing full, "start-to-finish" estimates for complex industrial facilities including Water/Wastewater Treatment, Pump Stations, and Power Substations.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

- Takeoff mastery: Perform detailed material and man-hour takeoffs for equipment, conduit (including PVC Coated GRS systems), wire, and 316SS supports and hangers, using ConEst IntelliBid.

- Design/Build Estimating: Quantify projects using incomplete drawing sets (30% or 60?sign level). You must be able to conceptualize and include missing information.

- Technical Review: Read and interpret P&IDs, Single Line Diagrams, and complex technical specifications.

- Retrofit Logic: Estimate sequencing, cutovers, temporary power, and generator work for industrial upgrades.

- Scope Development: Formulate clear scope clarifications and exclusions.



REQUIRED TECHNICAL EXPERTISE:

- Systems: Deep knowledge of 15kv, 5kv, 480V, 120V systems.

- Equipment: Experience with MCCs, VFDs, Switchgear, Controls Panels, etc.

- Process Controls: Strong understanding of water/wastewater instrumentation (flowmeters, analyzers, pressure transmitters, level transmitters, float switches, PLC's, RTU's, etc.)

- Hazardous Locations: Mastery of installation methods for corrosive and Classified/Hazardous environments.



WORK SCHEDULE & COMPENSATION:

Hours: 7:00 AM – 3:30 PM PHT (Allows for evening coordination with our California team).

Monthly Salary: $1,200 – $2,000 USD (Negotiable based on depth of Industrial & IntelliBid experience).

Benefits: 13th-Month Pay provided annually; stable, long-term remote work.



HOW TO APPLY:

To ensure you have read this entire post, please include the following in your application:

- Resume (PDF Preferred)

- A summary of your experience with ConEst IntelliBid and Industrial Electrical Estimating.

- A description of the most complex industrial electrical estimate you performed.

- Provide an answer to the following question: You are performing a 60% takeoff and the P&ID shows a flowmeter, but the Electrical Plan doesn't show the conduit or the PLC input. In IntelliBid, do you carry that as a 'Scope Clarification' or do you plug in a 'Typical Assembly' to cover it? Why?

title: Digital Marketing Manager/Strategist (remote)
description:
AM Eyewear, Australia’s Longest-Running Independent Fashion Eyewear Brand

We’re looking for a highly skilled Digital Marketing Manager to join AM Eyewear part time and help drive the next stage of our growth. If you live and breathe digital, love fashion, and want to work with a brand that actually values premium design and smart strategy, this one’s for you. (THIS IS NOT A SOCIAL MEDIA MANAGEMENT ROLE)

This role sits at the centre of our marketing engine, leading strategy and coordinating our Meta and Klaviyo specialists, managing campaigns end-to-end, and keeping everything aligned with performance goals and brand standards.

This position begins at 10 hours per week, with an hourly rate ranging from PHP 600 to 1,200. We offer regular performance reviews, with the opportunity to adjust the rate in line with demonstrated results.

Agencies do not apply.

However, if you currently work at an agency and want to take on a premium client after hours, we’d love to hear from you.

What You’ll Be Doing

• Oversee and execute AM Eyewear’s digital marketing strategy across Meta, Google, Klaviyo and other key channels.

• Liaise with our Meta media buyer and Klaviyo specialist to plan, coordinate and optimise campaigns.

• Brief creatives, campaign angles and performance insights to our design team.

• Manage the rollout of new product pushes, seasonal campaigns, and brand launches.

• Analyse performance data, prepare weekly reports for the Founder, and recommend improvements.

• Keep a close eye on trends in fashion, luxury, and content to ensure we’re always culturally on-point.

• Maintain strict brand consistency across digital channels.

What We’re Looking For

• 3+ years experience in digital marketing (e-commerce preferred).

• Strong understanding of Meta Ads, Google Ads, Klaviyo, CRO, and analytics.

• Someone who can think strategically, not just execute tasks.

• Excellent communication skills, you’ll be coordinating multiple specialists.

• A natural feel for premium aesthetics, fashion culture and luxury brand behaviour.

• Reliable, proactive and organised.

• Comfortable working autonomously and reporting directly to the Founder.

Nice to Have

• Experience working with eyewear, fashion, luxury or lifestyle brands.

• Basic design or content editing skills.

• Strong copywriting instincts.

 

What You’ll Love

• Working with a premium global eyewear brand known for quality, artistry and innovation.

• Flexible hours, ideal if you currently work at an agency.

• Direct access to the Founder, fast decisions, no corporate red tape.

• Long-term role with room to expand your responsibilities as the brand grows.

How to Apply

Send your CV, portfolio/case studies, and a short note on why you want to work with AM Eyewear to 
 .

title: Senior SEO Specialist
description:
Make sure you read this in full before applying:



Let me guess... You're the sharpest SEO mind in the room. But you're suffocating.



You're tired of presenting killer audit findings that get buried in a developer's "backlog." You’re tired of keyword-stuffed nonsense, begging for tool budgets, and explaining a 301 redirect to your boss for the tenth time.



This isn't just another job post. This is your exit strategy.



We’re not offering you a new title. We’re offering you a war room, an arsenal, and the targets to prove how good you really are.



Who Are We?

My name is Jim Ng. I built this agency on one principle: get clients tangible results, or get out of the way. 

In just a year, we've delivered for over 50 businesses. Now, we have a clear, audacious mission: to 10x our revenue in the next 12 months.



This isn’t a "hope and pray" strategy. It’s a battle plan. We need elite "Digital Warriors" who don't just "do SEO"—they command the search results.



The Stakes: We Put Our Skin in the Game

We don’t hide behind "SEO takes time" excuses. We offer our clients iron-clad guarantees:



- 10% of targeted keywords on Page 1 in 3 months.

- 30% of targeted keywords on Page 1 in 6 months.

- 60% of targeted keywords on Page 1 in 12 months.



Does this scare you, or does it excite you? If your first thought is "That's impossible," keep scrolling. If your first thought is "With my strategy and AI-driven efficiency, I can crush those numbers," then you’re the sniper we want.



What You’ll Actually Be Doing (The Blueprint)

- Architect of Success: You won't just advise on site structure; you’ll build the SEO Bible for our clients. You have the authority to see changes implemented.

- AI Command & Control: You use AI models not as a crutch, but as an exoskeleton. You engineer complex prompts to automate audits, generate schema at scale, and dismantle competitor strategies piece by piece.

- The Sherlock Holmes of Search: You conduct deep-dive technical, content, and backlink audits. You find the opportunities that Screaming Frog and Ahrefs miss because you know how to look beneath the data.

- Translate Data into Dollars: You live in GA4 and Search Console. You ignore vanity metrics. Your focus is singular: qualified organic traffic that drives business growth.



The Specialist’s Code (Is This You?)

- Growth & Mastery: You have 3+ years in the trenches. You see a Google Update as an opportunity to leapfrog the competition, not a crisis.

- Technical Dominance: You speak "developer." You understand CMS limitations, cloud hosting, and how to work with technical teams to get it right the first time.

- AI Power User: You are well-adept in prompt engineering. You know how to use AI to 10x your own output and accuracy.

- Future Leader: You want to be in a commanding position. You aren't looking for a job to hide in; you're looking for a platform to lead and eventually run your own pod.



The Real Pay-Off

- Unleashed Autonomy: You get the freedom to test, experiment, and implement. This is your domain to master.

- Remote Warfare: This is a 100% remote role. We care about the traffic graph moving up and to the right, not what chair you sit in.

- No Nightmare Clients: We protect our warriors. If a client won't implement your expert recommendations, we fire them.

- Empire Building: You are part of the core team. Your successes will be highly visible and critical to our 10x mission.



Ready to Enlist? Here’s where you can apply:





Nothing else will be entertained.

title: Senior Meta Ads Strategist
description:
Our vision is to build the Philippines’ most valuable consumer goods brands. By 2026, our goal is to become the #1 direct-to-consumer brand delivering premium, high-performance self-care solutions for busy, mature Filipino men.



To help us get there, we’re looking for a full-time Senior Meta Ads Strategist who takes true ownership of performance, thinks in systems, and thrives in a fast-paced, collaborative environment. In this role, you’ll plan, launch, optimize, and scale paid media campaigns with a clear focus on real business outcomes and not vanity metrics.



You’ll work closely with a tight-knit marketing team made up of creative team, influencer team, and marketing operations. Together, you’ll take video-ready creatives live, turn them into structured tests, identify scalable winners, and build repeatable growth systems. This role plays a key part in scaling brands that people don’t just buy from but know, trust, and remember.



If you’re analytical, decisive, comfortable balancing creative testing with performance accountability, and enjoy collaborating closely with creatives to get better ads live faster, you’ll feel right at home here.



Compensation & Benefits:



Salary: PHP 150,000 gross monthly pay with twice-a-year increases based on KPI performance    



Signing Bonus: PHP 100,000 = PHP 50,000 upon joining, remaining PHP 50,000 paid after successful completion of the 6-month probationary period. 



Bonuses: Performance-based bonuses and incentives for hitting or exceeding targets                     



Referral Bonus: PHP 50,000 for anyone who successfully refers a candidate that stays at least 3 months 



Benefits: Includes government-mandated benefits (SSS, PhilHealth, Pag-IBIG), 13th-month pay, and paid leave entitlements upon regularization



Contract: Full-time employee status following a 6-month probationary period, with regularization based on performance review







Job Responsibilities:



Strategic Ownership



Own the overall paid media strategy across Meta (TikTok/Google experience is a strong plus)



Define and evolve the testing framework: creative, audience, offer, and funnel tests    



Build monthly and quarterly scaling roadmaps tied to CM, CPA, and ROAS targets                           



Forecast spend, revenue, and efficiency targets and own the plan to hit them                              



Analyze long-term performance trends (60–90 day CPA, creative fatigue, seasonality) and adjust strategy

  proactively



Recommend new offers, bundles, pricing tests, and funnel experiments to improve conversion efficiency



Lead performance conversations with leadership and translate data into clear business decisions



Platform Ownership



Define and maintain the account structure strategy: campaign architecture, audience segmentation, and

 budget allocation framework



Own campaign setup, structure, and optimization across Meta                                           



Establish and enforce ad naming conventions, launch standards, and QA checklists                          



Ensure tracking, pixels, and events are firing correctly before and after launches                    



Flag account-level risks (spend spikes, learning resets, rejection patterns) and act on them



Daily Performance & Optimization



Monitor and optimize active campaigns daily (CPA, ROAS, CTR, CVR, CPM, delivery health, etc.)



Own the daily spend-to-CM connection and not just ad-level metrics but how today's performance tracks against monthly revenue targets



Be accountable for hitting agreed CPA, ROAS, spend, and revenue targets at scale        



Flag risks early and propose corrective actions when performance deviates



Make real-time decisions on scaling, pausing, and budget reallocation                                    



Manage pacing to hit daily and monthly spend targets



Identify and troubleshoot performance drops, delivery issues, and ad rejections          



Rotate creatives proactively to manage fatigue and sustain performance                  



Collaborate with the creative team to request and prioritize new assets based on performance gaps 



Make independent budget and optimization decisions within agreed guardrails                              



Daily reports summarizing observations, insights, and actions taken



Weekly Testing & Scaling



Define the testing roadmap and prioritize experiments based on expected business impact, not just creative volume                                                



Launch and manage structured weekly testing across hooks, creatives, angles, offers, and audiences



Run scaling experiments using CBO and ABO based on account stability



Analyze weekly performance trends across the full funnel (CPA, CTR, Hook/Hold Rate, CVR, AOV) 



Identify winning patterns across creative types, audiences, and offers. Build the playbook, not just  

  run tests                            



Maintain a clean, efficient ad account structure



Participate in regular performance syncs with ops, creatives, and leadership                              



Produce a clear weekly performance report: insights, winners/losers, spend allocation, and next steps



Provide clear, actionable performance feedback to creatives (what to iterate, why, and what to build next)



Align with creatives on 3–5 high-impact testing priorities for the week              



Propose funnel and landing page experiments to improve conversion efficiency



Monthly & Strategic Ownership



Lead monthly performance reviews with leadership: present what happened, why, and what changes next month



Own the winner pipeline health: track active winners, predict fatigue, and ensure new winners are being produced fast enough to sustain CM targets



Build and execute monthly scaling and testing roadmaps                                              



Analyze long-term performance trends and recommend strategic pivots before problems hit        



Improve media buying and creative workflows for speed and clarity                                          



Support creator briefing and creative sourcing initiatives with data-backed direction                



Detailed monthly reports summarizing performance, strategic learnings, and the plan forward



Cross-Functional Leadership



Partner with creative strategy to shape briefs informed by performance data. Close the loop between

  what's running and what gets built next                



Work with the influencer team to identify high-performing creator content and scale it          



Collaborate with ops to improve reporting, systems, and workflows                                          



Contribute to the team's growth by sharing frameworks, learnings, and best practices



KPIs/Outcomes:



Zero errors made during campaign, ad set, and ad launches



Achieves the Ad Spend / CPA / ROAS / CM goals set by leadership                                            



Maintains a healthy winner pipeline: 30+ active winners sustaining CM targets                          



Submits reports (daily, weekly, and monthly) on time with quality observations and action steps      



Leads monthly strategy presentations to leadership with clear recommendations



Qualifications: 



5+ years of proven experience managing and scaling paid media campaigns on Meta, with hands-on ownership of accounts spending ?8M+ monthly (TikTok and/or Google Ads is a strong plus)            



Track record of hitting revenue and contribution margin targets — not just managing CPA/ROAS in isolation, but understanding how paid media drives actual business outcomes                           



Experience building and refining media buying systems — testing frameworks, naming conventions, SOPs, reporting structures, and scalable workflows



Demonstrated ability to design structured testing programs that consistently produce winning ads and sustain a healthy pipeline of active winners



Strong strategic thinking — can analyze 60–90 day trends, diagnose systemic issues (creative fatigue, audience saturation, funnel leaks), and adjust the overall media strategy accordingly                



Experience leading performance conversations with leadership — can translate raw data into clear business recommendations and defend strategic decisions with confidence                                           



Has mentored or collaborated closely with creative and strategy teams, shaping briefs and feedback loops that directly improve ad performance



Deep understanding of full-funnel performance — not just ad-level metrics but landing pages, offers, AOV, and conversion efficiency



Comfortable making high-stakes budget and scaling decisions independently within agreed guardrails 



Strong grasp of tracking infrastructure — pixels, events, attribution, and basic troubleshooting across  

  ad platforms



Clear, structured communicator who documents learnings, frameworks, and next steps to build        

  institutional knowledge



High ownership, high accountability — treats the ad account like their own business                     



Required: Direct-response or performance-driven eCommerce experience. DTC brand experience is a strong plus.







Work Setup:







Contract: This is a full-time, employment position (starting with a 6 month probationary period)



Location: This is a fully remote position.



Hours: Monday to Friday, 9:00 AM to 7:00 PM PHT (includes a 1-hour break). While this role follows standard working hours, the Senior Meta Ads Strategist is expected to periodically monitor live ads outside of core hours for critical issues (e.g., delivery disruptions, rejected ads, sudden performance drops) and flag urgent concerns when needed.



Equipment: You must have a reliable internet connection and a suitable personal device, like a laptop, for work.

title: Client Relations / Virtual Accountant assistant
description:
VIRTUAL ASSISTANT

Property Management and Executive Support | Full-Time | Remote | 6-Month Trial Contract



We are seeking a detail-oriented and tech-savvy Virtual Assistant to support our property management and executive tasks. The ideal candidate will assist with phone management, tenant communication, document organization, calendar coordination, and web design projects. This role requires proficiency with Notion and excellent organizational skills to manage multiple tasks in a fast-paced environment.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES



Tenant Communication: Answer inquiries, coordinate property viewings, and assist with lease renewals.

Administrative Duties: Maintain property-related documents, handle invoicing, schedule maintenance requests, and generate reports.

Vendor Coordination: Source and manage contractors, track maintenance progress, and ensure timely service delivery.

Web Design Support: Update websites and maintain functionality as needed.

Executive Support: Manage
 correspondence, travel arrangements, and personal tasks for executives.





QUALIFICATIONS



Proven experience in property management tasks or executive assistance

Proficiency with Notion and familiarity with productivity tools

Experience with web design platforms such as WordPress or HTML

Excellent communication skills, written and verbal

Strong organizational skills with a proactive mindset

Ability to work independently in a remote setting and handle multiple priorities effectively





PREFERRED SKILLS



Familiarity with tenant management systems or CRM tools

Background in project management or marketing





CONTRACT TERMS

This is a full-time role starting with a 6-month trial contract. Upon successful completion and performance evaluation, the position may transition into a permanent role.



HOW TO APPLY

Read carefully. INCOMPLETE APPLICATIONS WILL NOT BE CONSIDERED.



Step 1: Record a short video (2 to 3 minutes) introducing yourself. In your video, tell us your name, your relevant experience with property management or executive support, and, so we know you read this entire posting, describe your favorite book and explain what lesson or idea from it you have actually applied in your own life. This does not need to be work-related.



Step 2: In your cover letter or message, start your first sentence with the phrase "I have experience with Notion and remote property management support." Applications that do not begin this way will not be reviewed.



Step 3: Include your resume with relevant experience highlighted, and if you have web design work or a portfolio, please include a link.

We review every application personally. Strong applicants will be invited to a short Zoom interview. We are looking for someone dependable who can grow into a long-term role with our team.

title: Virtual Assistant – Marketing & Operations   (EA + Marketing Coordination)
description:
About the Business



We are a purpose-led business supporting women to write powerful books, build meaningful businesses, and step into their identity as authors, leaders, and change-makers.



Behind the scenes, we operate with structure, precision, and high standards. Our systems, workflows, and execution are what allow the business to grow without chaos.



We are now looking for a Virtual Assistant – Marketing & Operations to support the day-to-day execution that keeps everything running smoothly.



---



About the Role



This is a part-time role (25 hours per week) focused on execution, coordination, and maintaining high standards across marketing and operations.



You will be responsible for ensuring that:



- Inbox and calendar are managed accurately  

- Webinars and launches are executed without error  

- Podcast and content are coordinated and scheduled on time  

- Tasks are completed consistently and to standard  



This role reports to the Operations Coordinator and works across marketing and operational workflows.



This is not a strategy role. It is a structured execution role for someone who is organised, detail-oriented, and reliable.



---



Core Responsibilities



Inbox & Communication Management  

- Manage and organise inbox daily  

- Respond to enquiries using templates  

- Flag priority
s requiring escalation  

- Maintain inbox structure and follow-ups  



Calendar & Scheduling  

- Manage bookings, reschedules, and calendar updates  

- Coordinate meetings, webinars, and calls  

- Set up Zoom links and manage access  

- Ensure correct links, time zones, and details  



Webinar & Launch Execution  

- Set up and manage:

  - Zoom links  

  - reminder
 sequences  

  - replay pages  

  - thank you pages  



- Duplicate and update
 sequences  

- Upload and manage webinar assets  

- Execute post-webinar tasks:

  - replay uploads  

  - follow-up
s  

  - list updates  



Podcast Coordination  

- Coordinate with Podcast VA (editing and production)  

- Track podcast pipeline in Asana  

- Ensure all assets are received on time  

- Manage approvals and revisions  

- Schedule podcast across platforms:

  - hosting platform  

  - YouTube  

  -
 (Klaviyo)  

  - social channels  



Content Scheduling & Marketing Execution  

- Schedule and publish pre-created content  

- Maintain social media content calendar  

- Upload captions, reels, and assets  

- Ensure content is accurate and on time  



Task Execution (Asana)  

- Execute assigned tasks in Asana  

- Update task status and progress  

- Ensure all tasks include:

  - clear objective and instructions (written and video where applicable)  

  - definition of done  

  - estimated time to complete  

  - owner, due date, and approval  



- Flag missing or unclear task details  



SOP & Process Adherence  

- Follow all documented SOPs  

- Update SOPs when processes change  

- Flag inefficiencies, gaps, or repeated issues  



General Admin Support  

- Upload and organise files  

- Manage forms, links, and backend systems  

- Support onboarding and general customer communication  



---



Role Structure



This is a 25-hour per week role.



Daily priorities are:



1. Inbox and calendar  

2. Time-sensitive tasks (webinars, scheduling)  

3. Task execution in Asana  

4. Podcast coordination and content scheduling  



---



Who This Role Is For



This role is ideal for someone who:



- Is highly organised and detail-oriented  

- Works well within structured systems and processes  

- Communicates clearly and professionally  

- Is reliable and consistent in execution  

- Can manage multiple tasks without losing accuracy  

- Is comfortable working across tools such as Asana,
 platforms, scheduling tools, and social media platforms  



This role suits someone who takes ownership of their work and values completing tasks properly.



---



What You’ll Be Working In



- A structured, process-driven environment  

- Clear expectations and defined workflows  

- A growing business with multiple moving parts  

- A team that values accuracy, consistency, and accountability  



---



Details



- Remote role  

- Part-time (25 hours per week)  

- Flexible hours, with some overlap with Australian business hours preferred  



---



How to Apply



Please include a short video (Loom or Google Drive link) answering:



1) Why this role appeals to you  

2) Your experience with admin, marketing coordination, or operations  

3) How you stay organised across multiple tasks and deadlines  

4) An example of a task or project where attention to detail was critical  

5) Your availability and hourly rate  



---



This role is suited to someone who values structure, consistency, and high-quality execution.



Strong systems and reliable delivery are what keep this business moving.

title: Short Term Property Appointment Setter (BASE + COMMISSION)
description:

We are seeking a highly motivated and articulate Appointment Setter to support our growing short-term rental and accommodation services division who is happy to work on a base salary + Commission



The primary purpose of this role is to conduct consistent outbound outreach to leads within our pipeline, qualify prospects, and convert cold leads into warm, sales-ready opportunities.



The ideal candidate must be highly proficient in spoken English, confident on the phone, and possess a strong understanding of short-term rental, serviced accommodation, and temporary housing requirements.



Key Responsibilities

Conduct daily outbound calls to cold and existing pipeline leads

Qualify prospects based on accommodation needs, timelines, budgets, and decision-making authority

Convert cold leads into warm, qualified appointments for the sales team

Book meetings between prospective clients and Business Development Managers

Follow up consistently with unresponsive or long-term leads

Maintain accurate CRM records and update lead status daily

Identify client requirements relating to short-term rental, decant accommodation, emergency housing, or corporate stays

Handle objections professionally and build rapport quickly

Meet and exceed individual KPIs and conversion targets

Collaborate with the sales team to improve outreach messaging and conversion strategies

Provide feedback on lead quality and pipeline performance

Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)

Minimum daily outbound call target

Number of meaningful conversations per day

Appointment booking rate

Cold-to-warm lead conversion rate

CRM accuracy and data completeness

Monthly qualified meetings target

Contribution to revenue pipeline value

Required Skills & Experience

Proven experience in appointment setting, telesales, or outbound lead generation

Excellent spoken and written English (mandatory)

Confident, clear, and professional telephone manner

Strong understanding of short-term rental, serviced accommodation, or housing sectors

Ability to quickly understand client accommodation requirements

Experience using CRM systems

Resilient, target-driven, and comfortable handling rejection

Strong time management and organisational skills



Desirable

Experience working with housing associations, councils, insurance providers, or corporate relocation clients

Knowledge of temporary accommodation procurement processes

Experience in B2B outreach

Background in property, lettings, or hospitality



THERE IS NO CAP ON COMMISSION, EXPECTED OTE $
USD PCM



Personal Attributes

Self-motivated and disciplined (especially if remote)

Persistent and results-oriented

Strong listening skills

Positive, professional attitude

Ability to work independently while contributing to team goals



What Success Looks Like

A consistently active and nurtured pipeline

High volume of quality appointments booked monthly

Clear conversion tracking from cold lead to sales opportunity

Strong client feedback on professionalism and communication



1. Resume in PDF format

2. Internet Speed test Result dated December 2024.

3. No less than TWO Professional Work References with name, your relationship with them, their
 address, and their mobile number.

4. Photo of your home office with your computer and all backups clearly visible.

* Incomplete responses shall not be entertained. Applications not sent to the
 provided above will also be rejected*

title: Customer Success Representative
description:
This is a highly personal, proactive customer success role — not a reactive support position. You won't wait for clients to come to you. You'll stay ahead of them, guide them through their courses and programs, and make sure they're moving forward every step of the way. If you genuinely love people and want to see them win, this role was made for you.



What You'll Do:



1. Client Success & Progress Tracking:

   a. Proactively monitor how each client is progressing across multiple program components, each with their own recurring timelines

   b. Reach out consistently — clients often won't ask for what they need, so you bring it to them

   c. Congratulate clients when they hit milestones

   d. Encourage and re-engage clients who fall behind



2. Onboarding:

   a. Onboard new clients into the program

   b. Set up their GHL account and get them fully started

   c. Make them feel welcomed and clear on next steps from day one



3. 6-Week Accelerator Support:

   a. Ensure clients show up each week

   b. Make sure they have the right information to complete the work

   c. Coach them through each stage of the accelerator — think of yourself as their guide, not just their rep



4. Issue Resolution:

   a. You are not responsible for fixing technical or program issues, but you are responsible for identifying them and getting the right tea
mber to resolve them quickly

   b. Be the client's advocate internally



Who You Are:

> You genuinely care about people and their success — this isn't just a job task, it's a personality trait

> You get to know each client personally and advocate on their behalf

> You're proactive, not reactive — you follow through without being asked



Skills:

+ Outstanding communication — warm, clear, and consistent

+ Highly organized with strong follow-through

+ Comfortable working across
, SMS/Text, and Zoom

+ Able to manage multiple clients and timelines simultaneously



A Note on This Role: 

This is a part-time role. Also, this is a new program, which means the communication systems and workflows are still being built. You'll play a key role in helping shape how client communication runs. If you thrive in a building environment and enjoy creating structure, this is a great opportunity.



Please answer the following when you apply:

1. Tell us about a time you proactively reached out to a client or customer before they knew they needed help. What happened and what was the outcome?

2. How do you personally stay organized when managing multiple clients or projects with different timelines? Walk us through your system.

3. This role requires a lot of heart — you'll be celebrating wins and nudging people who are falling behind. How do you naturally show up for people, and why does that matter to you?

4. Is $5/hr acceptable for you?

title: Proofreading | Content Enhancer
description:
[Caution]

If you are a slow reader, this job may become a torture for you, so I'd like you to beware.

But if you're a fast reader with keen eyes for the details, this can be a fun part time job for you.



Also, if you were going to use AI for this task, please step out. I discovered that you cannot use AI tools for this particular task, as you'd need to pick the best sentence among the 5 versions of the same context for each sentence one by one. I've tried this task with AI myself, and trust me it's my current best friend, but it cannot give me the result at the human level that I'd like. So, no AIs period.



So, only read further if you are confident in your ability to consistently select the most effective phrasing to build a high-quality, engaging video script.



This role requires a keen eye for detail and a strong sense of narrative flow. If you have a passion for reading long scripts and enjoy learning about storytelling techniques while phrasing impactful sentences, this position will be a great fit.



This job relies on your human judgment to discern which sentence best serves the story and engages the viewer. The goal is to create a script that is clear, compelling, and flows seamlessly from one idea to the next.





[Job Title]

Proofreading - selecting best sentences among 5 variations





[Work Type]

Remote | Flexible Hours | Long-term Contract





[Base Payment]

You’ll receive $100 USD per 10 project.

Currently, the other assistant gets each project done in 3-4 hours.

I can provide 3-7 projects per week, depending on your speed.

Each project involves working on a script for my video (each project = around
words in English).





[Bonus]

$100 USD commitment bonus after completing 30 projects (you will be paid $100+$100 for every 30th project you complete)

This means your compensation for each project will be $13.33 including the base + bonus payment.





[What You’ll Be Doing]



Your primary task is to assemble the best possible version of a video script by choosing the most effective sentence from a set of options.



For a script of approximately
words, each sentence will be presented with five different variations. Your job is to read through all five options and select the one that works best in the context of the paragraph and the overall script. You will repeat this process for every sentence until the entire script is complete.



Here’s a step-by-step process for clarity:



Step 1. Understand the Context - You will receive a complete script where each sentence is accompanied by five alternative versions.



Step 2. Select the Best Sentences - Choose the option that offers the best clarity, flow, and impact. Your selection should contribute to a cohesive and engaging narrative.



Step 3. Double Check - Once you have made a selection for every sentence, read through the completed script one last time to ensure it is coherent and polished.



Step 4. Submit the final script.



*Note:

-The first project may take a bit longer as we align on tone and style with feedback and revisions. I’ll provide detailed feedback on your first completed script to help guide that alignment.



-I intend to reduce the revisions down to 1, but initially we may need more.



-With each run, you will become more familiar with the content style, and the selection process will become faster and more intuitive on your end.





[Goal]



The goal is to craft a highly engaging and easy-to-follow script by meticulously selecting the best version of every sentence. This process ensures the final script is polished and holds the audience's attention from start to finish.



This method is designed to be more efficient than rewriting from scratch. Instead of spending time guessing my intended tone, you can focus entirely on choosing the strongest lines from pre-written options, which keeps the process focused and effective.





[What We’re Looking For]

The ideal candidate:



-Has an advanced or native level of written English and a strong grasp of grammar, idioms, and slang. This is crucial for quickly and accurately judging which phrases are most effective.



-Is coachable and eager to learn.



-Communicates effectively and values mutual respect.



-Has strong time management skills to meet deadlines.



-Is accountable and committed to the role.



-Can collaborate with me or our team to ensure the content’s vibe aligns with our creative vision.



*Important Note:

We value respectful communication. If you need to step away, just let me or your manager know when. Your feedback also helps us improve. We understand life happens, even if you need to leave tomorrow, but ghosting causes confusion and delays. Our team thrives on mutual respect, no matter your role.





[Why Work With Us?]

-Flexible hours to suit your lifestyle.

-A supportive environment that celebrates creativity and input.





[About Us]

We help YouTube creators master storytelling techniques to captivate audiences and achieve viral success. Your role will be key in polishing our content and empowering creators worldwide.





[MUST READ]

In your application, include:

1. Start your response with the following word at the top: "IREADFAST"

2. Experience or Work History: Share any relevant experience in proofreading, editing, or writing.

3. Why you’re Interested: Tell us why you’re excited about this opportunity.

4. Available Hours: Let us know your general availability each day of the week (For example: Monday: 11:00 AM - 2:00 PM, Tuesday: 3:30 PM - 8:00 PM, etc.).

5. Available Period of Work: Let us know for how long you expect to be available for this job. We prefer a commitment of at least 3 months.

6. Your Internet Availability: Let us know your monthly internet data limit (e.g., GBs or Unlimited).

7. Willingness for Zoo
etings: Confirm if you are willing to participate in occasional Zoo
etings to align on goals and build team synergy.



I will respond to all applications within 7-15 days.



-----Job Posting Ends here-----









[Sample of what the task will look like]

*2025_11_28 Note:

This is the proofreading task we worked on earlier, which has already been turned into a video.

The proofreader was asked to choose the best version among the 4 on their judgement.



((Script begins here))



### **[Body Section 1 - on T&T - with humorous lines]**



[Original phrase]: Section Title: The Attention Bomb: Engineering a Title & Thumbnail That Commands the Click

[Refined phrase v1]: Section Title: The Scientific Click: Engineering a Title & Thumbnail That’s Impossible to Ignore

[Refined phrase v2]: Section Title: The Authority Formula: How to Engineer a Title & Thumbnail That Gets the Click Without Selling Your Soul

[Refined phrase v3]: Section Title: The Ethical Clickbait: How to Engineer a Title & Thumbnail That's Both Irresistible and Honest



***



[Original phrase]: On YouTube, you don't get a second chance to make a first impression.

[Refined phrase v1]: On YouTube, your first impression is your only impression.

[Refined phrase v2]: On YouTube, the viewer judges your video’s cover in about 0.3 seconds. No pressure.

[Refined phrase v3]: On YouTube, your thumbnail is on trial and the viewer is the judge, jury, and executioner.



***



[Original phrase]: Your title and thumbnail are your digital handshake, your movie poster, and your opening line all rolled into one.

[Refined phrase v1]: Your title and thumbnail are your video's entire resume, and the viewer is the hiring manager with a zero-second attention span.

[Refined phrase v2]: Your title and thumbnail are your video’s dating profile. The viewer is swiping, and they will judge you based on one photo and the bio.

[Refined phrase v3]: Your title and thumbnail are the book cover. And everyone, absolutely everyone, judges a book by its cover.



***



[Original phrase]: Most creators get it wrong.

[Refined phrase v1]: And most creators fail the interview.

[Refined phrase v2]: And most creators get swiped left.

[Refined phrase v3]: And most creators stay on the shelf.



And most creators get it wrong.





***



[Original phrase]: They either scream for attention with hyperbolic clickbait ("MY INSANE TRANSFORMATION!") or they're so boring they become invisible ("My Fitness Journey").

[Refined phrase v1]: They either scream for attention with exaggerated clickbait ("30 DAYS TO EXPLODE MY BICEP!") or they're so boring they become invisible ("Leg Day Vlog #72").

[Refined phrase v2]: They’re either the person at the party yelling “LOOK AT ME!” or the person in the corner who is so quiet they get mistaken for a coat rack.

[Refined phrase v3]: They're either dressed in a neon-yellow suit lighting fireworks, or they're wearing camouflage.



***



[Original phrase]: But in his viral video, “How Much Muscle Did I Gain In 365 Days? (Scientific Experiment)”, Jeff Nippard doesn't shout, and he isn't boring.

[Refined phrase v1]: But Jeff Nippard doesn't use hype, and he isn't boring.

[Refined phrase v2]: But Jeff Nippard doesn't yell, and he isn't a coat rack. He’s the interesting person in the middle that everyone wants to talk to.

[Refined phrase v3]: Jeff isn't wearing a neon suit or camouflage. He's wearing a perfectly tailored suit.



***



[Original phrase]: He engineers a question so compelling that clicking becomes an impulse.

[Refined phrase v1]: He engineers a question so interesting that clicking on it feels like a reflex.

[Refined phrase v2]: He crafts a question so magnetic that your mouse cursor starts moving towards it.

[Refined phrase v3]: He creates a question so good it feels like your brain has an allergy to not knowing the answer.



***



[Original phrase]: This is his first strategy, and it’s a masterclass: Don’t make a claim, create a contract.

[Refined phrase v1]: This is his first strategy: Don’t be a salesman making a pitch. Be a scientist proposing an experiment the viewer can't help but want to see the results of.

[Refined phrase v2]: This is his first move, and it's brilliant: Don't make a claim that can be dismissed. Ask a question that must be answered.

[Refined phrase v3]: This is the core of it: Don't write an advertisement. Write a summons.



***



[Original phrase]: Let's break down the two elements of this contract.

[Refined phrase v1]: Let's analyze the two parts of this contract.

[Refined phrase v2]: Let's pop the hood and look at the two key parts of this engine.

[Refined phrase v3]: Let's look at the two key ingredients in this recipe.



***



[Original phrase]: Part 1: The Title - A Testable Promise

[Refined phrase v1]: Part 1: The Title, which acts as a The Official Research Question

[Refined phrase v2]: Part 1: The Title, which acts as the 'Research Question'.

[Refined phrase v3]: Part 1: The Title, which is the Central Mystery.



***



[Original phrase]: Jeff's title isn't a statement; it's a hypothesis. Let's dissect it:

[Refined phrase v1]: Jeff's title isn't a statement; it's a hypothesis. Let's dissect it.

[Refined phrase v2]: Jeff’s title isn’t a statement; it’s the setup to a mystery. Let’s look for clues.

[Refined phrase v3]: Jeff's title isn't a statement; it's the opening line of a cliffhanger. Let's see how the episode ends.



***



[Original phrase]: The Core Question: "How Much Muscle Did I Gain..."

[Refined phrase v1]: First, the Core Question: "How Much Muscle Did I Gain..."

[Refined phrase v2]: First, the Mystery Itself: "How Much Muscle Did I Gain..."

[Refined phrase v3]: First, The Unanswered Variable: "How Much Muscle Did I Gain..."



***

(continues this way until the end of the script)

title: Senior Laravel Developer (Laravel + React + Inertia)
description:
SixSides is hiring a full-time remote Senior Laravel Developer to help build our event platform.



We build modern software for conferences, communities, and live events. Our platform powers event websites, admin tools, APIs, mobile app experiences, attendee engagement features, and integrations that help organisers run better events.



This role is primarily focused on Laravel and React. You do not need to know every part of our stack, but you should be very strong with Laravel and comfortable contributing on the React side. Experience with Cloudflare, Expo, and the wider platform stack is a bonus.



You’ll be working with PHP, Laravel, React, AWS, Lambda, Cloudflare, Terraform, PlanetScale, Cursor or equivalent AI coding tools, and PhpStorm. Exposure to React Native, Expo, and Cloudflare Workers is a plus.



We are looking for someone who can build product features properly, write clean maintainable code, work independently, and communicate clearly. This is a senior role within a small team, so we want someone who can take ownership of their work and contribute thoughtfully, not just complete tasks blindly.



In this role, you will work on backend systems, APIs, product features, integrations, internal tools, and platform improvements across SixSides. You will help design and build new features, improve existing parts of the system, fix bugs, and help keep the codebase clean and scalable as the product grows.



You should have strong commercial experience with Laravel and PHP, solid experience working with React, confidence building and maintaining APIs, and a good understanding of modern web application architecture. You should be comfortable working remotely without constant supervision, able to communicate clearly in written English, and open to using AI tools as part of your workflow.



Bonus points if you have experience with AWS, Lambda, Terraform, Cloudflare, PlanetScale, React Native, Expo, serverless architecture, infrastructure as code, or supporting production applications at scale.



This is a full-time remote role, 40 hours per week, with a salary of 75,000 PHP per month.



To apply, please send your CV or resume, a short introduction about yourself, links to any relevant GitHub or past work, and a short summary of your Laravel and React experience.

title: Product Sourcing Specialist (Dropshipping)
description:
BrandFi Inc. is looking for a detail-oriented Product Sourcing Specialist who can identify profitable products and reliable suppliers for our dropshipping operations.



This role requires strong research ability, supplier negotiation skills, and a deep understanding of pricing, shipping logistics, and product quality.



We are not looking for someone who simply searches AliExpress. We need someone who understands how to find high-margin products, negotiate supplier pricing, and manage shipping efficiency.



?



Role Overview



The Product Sourcing Specialist will be responsible for finding products that can be sold profitably while maintaining strong supplier relationships and reliable shipping timelines.



You will identify suppliers, compare pricing across multiple platforms, negotiate costs when possible, and ensure shipping reliability.



The goal is to maintain high margins while ensuring product quality and fulfillment reliability.



?



Key Responsibilities



Product Research & Sourcing



• Identify profitable products based on market demand and competition

• Source products from reliable suppliers

• Compare supplier pricing across multiple platforms

• Ensure product quality and supplier reliability



Supplier & Price Optimization



• Negotiate product pricing where possible

• Identify suppliers offering the best wholesale rates

• Monitor product cost changes and adjust sourcing strategy



Shipping & Fulfillment Management



• Identify suppliers with reliable shipping times

• Evaluate fulfillment options (AliExpress, CJ Dropshipping, private suppliers, etc.)

• Ensure shipping timelines meet customer expectations



Product Validation



• Evaluate product quality and supplier ratings

• Verify supplier reliability and fulfillment capability

• Check order processing times and return policies



Cost & Margin Analysis



• Ensure products maintain healthy profit margins

• Factor in product cost, shipping cost, and platform fees

• Recommend pricing strategies to maintain competitiveness



?



Daily Responsibilities



• Research and identify new product opportunities

• Compare supplier pricing and shipping options

• Build product sourcing lists with cost breakdowns

• Verify supplier reliability and fulfillment speed

• Maintain organized sourcing spreadsheets and supplier databases



?



Required Skills & Experience



• Experience with dropshipping product sourcing

• Strong supplier research skills

• Experience evaluating product margins

• Understanding of shipping logistics and fulfillment timelines

• Ability to identify reliable suppliers and avoid low-quality vendors

• Strong attention to detail



?



Preferred Experience



• Experience with Shopify stores

• Experience sourcing from AliExpress, CJ Dropshipping, or private suppliers

• Experience negotiating supplier pricing

• Understanding of product trend analysis



?



Work Style Expectations



• Highly detail-oriented

• Research-driven and analytical

• Comfortable comparing suppliers and negotiating pricing

• Process-driven and organized



?



Important Notes



• This role requires real research and supplier evaluation

• We prioritize quality suppliers and strong margins

• Candidates must be comfortable performing detailed product analysis
